description: Ficus Audrey (Ficus benghalensis), the Indian Banyan tree, is a popular houseplant that is mildly toxic to cats. Like other Ficus species, it produces a milky latex sap containing proteolytic enzymes (ficin) and irritant compounds. These cause gastrointestinal upset when ingested and contact dermatitis when the sap touches skin.
name: Ficus Audrey
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: ficin (proteolytic enzyme in latex sap), irritant compounds
Mechanism: ficin directly irritates mucous membranes and the GI tract; latex contact causes localised skin inflammation
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, drooling, loss of appetite; skin contact with broken stems or leaves may cause redness or rash
Symptoms are generally mild and self-limiting; seek vet attention if vomiting is severe or prolonged
Wipe away sap from cat skin promptly; the plant is most hazardous when stems are broken and latex oozes

symptoms.name: Mild Oral Irritation (burning sensation in mouth/tongue)
symptoms.onset: Immediate (Upon chewing or contact with sap).
